title: NOC 72103 – Boilermakers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72103 professionals are the heavy-metal specialists of the industrial world. As a TEER 2 occupation, Boilermakers fabricate, assemble, erect, and repair the massive boilers, tanks, and vats that power Canada’s most critical sectors—from nuclear power generation to shipbuilding. In 2026, as Canada executes a multi-billion dollar overhaul of its energy grid and industrial infrastructure, Boilermakers have been designated as "Critical Infrastructure Talent." For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Express Entry Category-Based Selection.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72103

duties: Fabrication & Layout: Read blueprints and specifications to plan the sequence of operations; lay out plate and sheet steel, marking bending and cutting lines using precision instruments.
Machine Operation: Set up and operate heavy-metal working machines such as brakes, rolls, shears, and flame cutters to shape metal sections.
Assembly: Fit and weld metal parts or sections together to fabricate boilers, pressure vessels, tanks, and heat exchangers.
Installation: Erect and install boilers and other heavy-metal products according to specifications using rigging, hoisting, and power tools.
Maintenance & Repair: Perform essential maintenance and repair work on existing boiler systems to ensure safety and efficiency.
Testing: Conduct hydro-testing and other verification methods to ensure the structural integrity of finished vessels.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 3-to-4-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over 4 years of work experience and industry-certified courses.

Certification (Trade Seal): * Trade certification is compulsory in Alberta, Quebec, and Nova Scotia.Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.Obtaining the Red Seal Endorsement is highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and is a major asset for PR applications.
Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.
Obtaining the Red Seal Endorsement is highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and is a major asset for PR applications.
Specialized Status: Boilermakers working in nuclear facilities in 2026 are often required to obtain "Nuclear-Qualified Status."
salary_prospects: Note: Due to the nature of the work, many Boilermakers earn annual incomes exceeding $110,000 - $135,000 when factoring in specialized overtime, travel premiums, and shutdown bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector will face a steady need for new workers due to the transition to net-zero energy and the expansion of the shipbuilding industry (National Shipbuilding Strategy). The requirement for specialized pressure vessel fabrication for hydrogen storage and carbon capture systems ensures long-term demand through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Boilermakers is Good to Very Good, with acute shortages reported in industrial corridors. In 2026, Ontario (specifically the Bruce Peninsula and Clarington regions) and Alberta (Edmonton/Fort McMurray) are the primary hiring hubs. The demand is heavily driven by Nuclear Life-Extension projects and the construction of new Clean-Tech manufacturing facilities. With a high percentage of the workforce approaching retirement, the Canadian government has listed Boilermakers as a priority trade for 2026.
Industrial Scale Projects: Work on some of the largest engineering projects in North America, including the refurbishment of world-class nuclear reactors.
Specialized High Wages: Boilermaking is consistently among the highest-paid trades in the "Heavy Industrial" sector, with significant premiums for "Shutdown" or "Turnaround" contracts.
Technological Integration: Manage automated welding systems and digital non-destructive testing (NDT) equipment.
Strategic PR Pathway: This role is explicitly prioritized in Category-Based Express Entry draws for "Skilled Trades," often allowing for lower CRS cut-offs.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary federal route for tradespeople with at least 2 years of experience.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): Ideal for those with a job offer in Ontario’s heavy manufacturing or energy sector.
Nova Scotia NP (Skilled Worker): A fast-track route for Boilermakers supporting the province's shipbuilding and industrial hubs.
